Article.ArticleTitle: Response: Reply to the commentary on our study about respiratory muscle strength in stroke patients.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Sakarya University of Applied Sciences, Department of Physiotherapy and Rehabilitation - Sakarya, Türkiye.
Istanbul University-Cerrahpaşa, Faculty of Health Sciences, Department of Physiotherapy and Rehabilitation - Istanbul, Türkiye.
University of Health Sciences, Istanbul Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation Training and Research Hospital - Istanbul, Türkiye.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Conflicts of interest: the authors declare there is no conflicts of interest.

Reference.Citation: Finsterer J. Before respiratory muscle weakness is attributed to a stroke, alternative causes must be considered and thoroughly ruled out. Rev Assoc Med Bras (1992) 2025;71(3):e20241422. doi: 10.1590/1806-9282.20241422.

Evaluation of respiratory muscle strength in the acute phase of stroke: the role of aging and anthropometric variables. J Stroke Cerebrovasc Dis. 2017;26(10):2300–2305. doi: 10.1016/j.jstrokecerebrovasdis.2017.05.014.

Dickstein R. Rehabilitation of gait speed after stroke: a critical review of intervention approaches. Neurorehabil Neural Repair. 2008;22(6):649–660. doi: 10.1177/1545968308315997.

Sens E, Knorr C, Preul C, Meissner W, Witte OW, Miltner WH, et al. Differences in somatosensory and motor improvement during temporary functional deafferentation in stroke patients and healthy subjects. Behav Brain Res. 2013;252:110–116. doi: 10.1016/j.bbr.2013.05.048.
